title: Alan Wake

notes: Running out of the box at 35-45 fps without sound in cutscenes, but when i installed d9vk(0.12) i got 50-65 fps without any artefacts or bugs and with sound in everywhere. There is how to install d9vk: download d9vk from https://github.com/Joshua-Ashton/d9vk/releases/tag/0.12 or some new release and unarchive that > install, run game and close it > open console and write export WINEPREFIX='~/.steam/steam/steamapps/compatdata/108710/' , then go into d9vk folder(cd command) and run ./setup_dxvk.sh install - thats all. Also i recommend you to disable motion blur: download VectorBlur.obj from http://www.mediafire.com/?656m22iee02q2h3 and replace it in {game folder}/shaders/build/pc/
title: Alan Wake

notes: To make sound work set launch parameters in steam to DXVK_HUD=1 %command% . Without this small, but important additional to my previous review there is no sound in cutscenes.
title: Alan Wake

notes: Works flawlessly using Gallium Nine standalone (no glitches or corrupted textures/ilumination). As previously mentioned, "DXVK_HUD=1 %command%" fixes cutscenes audio.
title: Alan Wake

notes: Important update: previous fix ("DXVK_HUD=1 %command%") now BREAKS cutscene audio. Works natively so far.

notes: d9vk realy up performance! But, the videos are silent, and the TVs that show "nihtsprings" reproduce only the sound, but otherwise - perfect.

notes: In my case the game seems to run pretty nicely. Performance is not so good (I'd say 25-40 fps depending on the configuration) Not sure exactly why, I used to have a 570 GTX in the old slave windows times, which performed better. // The main glitch is a graphical issue where some vegetal textures seems to be non-transparent, the grass looks like papers or something like that. I have tried every Proton version since 3.16 to see if it is fixed, but It still isn't.

title: Alan Wake

notes: Ran fantastic on high settings (90+ FPS) with d9vk. I want to give this a gold, but there is one big issue: the game often crashes when I try to start it, it often takes several times before the game opens. But once it does, it works perfect, including controller support.
title: Alan Wake

notes: I previously reported it as Silver, but it does play way better now. It is almost Platinum actually. There is no more graphical glitches, or flickering textures and shadows. I have a newer Proton and Mesa, so hopefully that was it. I tested various game features, played a bit of Episode 1, and later episode, and checked all the options, collectibles, etc. It all works. The only minor glitch is mouse acceleration is somehow still not perfect, and makes control a bit hard, but it is better than before! Also the "moon/sun follows the view vertically" bug, is still present. Really weird, I know others people also experienced it, but it doesn't really affect the game in any significant way. The game is fully playable and works very smoothly, and I couldn't spot any other issues.
title: Alan Wake

notes: Takes 2-5 retries to start the game. Runs perfectly with d9vk; no more graphical glitches/crashes even the cutscenes-audio works.
title: Alan Wake

notes: I am running the epic store's version of game. Initially there were many audio issue. Which i was able to solve by the DXVK_HUD command and changing the Vulkan icd loader to amd.json.
title: Alan Wake

notes: Game works perfectly with "PROTON_USE_D9VK=1 %command%" launch option. Without it there are graphical glitches.
title: Alan Wake

notes: No graphical glitches, runs with great performance, sound is now crystal clear, but still missing in cut scenes. No crashes just like in the previous proton versions. If the audio in the cut scenes wouldn't be important for games feeling I would rate gold.
title: Alan Wake

notes: Game took a few tries to start and when it did, was able to run the game on high with the PROTON_USE_D9VK=1 %command% and a PS4 controller. I am marking this down due to the issues in launching the game and mouse movement causing minor shuddering and drop in FPS while a controller with gyro was fine.
title: Alan Wake
